name: Chapel
description: Small intimate religious room: a tall, narrow chamber with an altar on a raised dais against the far short wall, a central carpet aisle, two or three rows of pews facing the altar, tall stained-glass windows punched through the long side walls, a hanging lantern chandelier at the nave centre, and wall torches between the windows. Ceiling is either a flat slab lid or, when room allows, a small stepped vault of accent blocks over the centre.

required_furniture.rule: A 2 wide × 1 tall × 1 deep Fill of @accent against the far short wall (z = z0+1), centered on the room's x axis. Topped with a single minecraft:enchanting_table on the centre cell of the dais.
Rows of @stairs[facing=north] on the floor (y0+1), spanning the interior width minus the aisle column, evenly spaced along z between the altar and the entrance. 3 rows when depth ≥ 12, else 2.
A cluster of minecraft:lantern[hanging=true] one block below the ceiling lid, arranged in a + pattern around the room centre. Always at least 3 lanterns total.
minecraft:wall_torch blocks on the interior faces of the long (east + west) walls, oriented away from the wall, placed between the stained-glass window columns. 2-3 per side.
A single 1-wide @carpet runner one block above the floor, centred on x, running from the entrance row (z1-2) to the foot of the altar dais (z0+2).
Tall 2-3 block vertical columns of @glass punched through the east and west long walls, evenly distributed along z. Height = 3 when room height ≥ 6, else 2. 2 or 3 windows per side.

placement_rules: Altar at far end, raised on dais
Tall narrow windows on side walls
Chandelier hangs above the nave centre
Wall torches sit between the window columns, never overlap them
lighting_ventilation: Layered lighting: hanging lantern chandelier at the conceptual nave centre below the ceiling, wall torches on the long walls between windows, and tall stained-glass windows on both side walls flood the nave with coloured daylight. Tall room (≥ 5 high) keeps the volume vertical and contemplative.

medieval.notes: Romanesque chapel: oak pews, stone-brick walls, red stained glass, oak floor; the enchanting table reads as a stone altar.

modern.notes: Minimalist civic chapel: quartz + smooth stone, white glazing, recessed lantern chandelier; pew rows in smooth-stone stairs read as benches.

fantasy.notes: Dark-oak shrine with purpur altar dais, purple stained-glass windows, sea-lantern chandelier (overrides the @light slot); the enchanting table reads as an arcane focus.
